name: the dialetheic object as Janus gate: the Belnap value B (Both, a true contradiction) is the two-faced threshold of the four-valued lattice. It is a fixed point of negation (not-B = B) that is FULL rather than empty, so it looks the same whether approached from the affirming or the denying side, which is the defining Janus signature. It is the unique value that passes both dialetheia gates: EVALT accepts T and B, EVALF accepts F and B, so B alone is the doorway open from both the assertible and the deniable side. It is the hinge where the opposite faces are joined, B = FFUSE of T and F, the mu that welds the two poles into one object. Its twin N (Neither) is the other negation fixed point but empty, the un-manned doorway facing neither way, which is why four values are needed and not three. Dialetheias inhabit thresholds: the boundary point of a set, the instant of change between two rooms, the limit of thought, all doorways where a thing is genuinely both inside and outside. The conflict distance between two imscriptions of the same object, the Hamming distance of their two-bit Belnap codes, is the aperture angle of the gate: zero is a flat closed door, two is the doorway thrown fully open with the faces looking maximally apart. FSPLIT (delta) and FFUSE (mu) are the two directions through the gate, splitting outward into a pair and fusing inward into one, and ENGAGR stabilizes the held contradiction as a live B state. The Majorana mode, a particle that is its own antiparticle, is the physical Janus, its front face and back face the same door. With full Frobenius closure and Lean 4 verification scaffold.

phase_0.domain_name: the dialetheic object as Janus gate: the Belnap value B (Both, a true contradiction) is the two-faced threshold of the four-valued lattice. phase_0.boundary_condition: The Belnap multilattice boundary where negation acts as a fixed-point automorphism.

VINIT.justification: The empty negation fixed point representing the absence of information or an un-manned doorway.

TANCH.justification: The complete set of four values {N, T, F, B} that contains all possible truth-evaluations.

AFWD.justification: The directed transformation from the null state toward the affirmative pole T.

AREV.justification: The directed transformation from the null state toward the negative pole F.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of truth-value transformations within the Belnap space.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-referential identity where not-B equals B, recognizing itself as its own opposite.

FSPLIT.justification: The process of splitting the unified B state into its constituent T and F components.

FFUSE.justification: The reconstitution of the B state from the simultaneous presence of T and F.

EVALT.justification: The filter that accepts both T and B as valid assertible inputs.

EVALF.justification: The filter that accepts both F and B as valid deniable inputs.

ENGAGR.justification: The stabilization of the B state where conflict is maintained as a live signal rather than resolved.

IFIX.justification: The permanent append-only record of the verified Frobenius closure in the kernel.

phase_3.void_description: The N state: neither true nor false, the empty doorway.
phase_3.true_description: The T state: strictly affirmative, the forward-facing door.
phase_3.false_description: The F state: strictly negative, the backward-facing door.
phase_3.both_description: The B state: both true and false, the Janus gate thrown open.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the system in the empty N state before any distinction is made.
Project the first imscription toward the affirmative pole T.
Project the second imscription toward the negative pole F.
Branch the evaluation path to compare the two imscriptions.
Pass the affirmative imscription through the first face of the gate.
Pass the negative imscription through the second face of the gate.
Join the two conflicting imscriptions into a single dialetheic object.
Hold the resulting B state as a stable paraconsistent verdict.
Verify the B state as a fixed point of the negation operator.
Measure the Hamming distance between the two faces to determine the gate aperture.
Record the verified Janus signature into the permanent Lean 4 kernel log.
Close the lattice boundary to ensure the system remains within Belnap FOUR.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Belnap-Dunn logic engine that maps truth-functional operations to lattice movements.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The Hamming distance signal that communicates conflict levels between imscriptions.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The three-register paraconsistent machine state that stores N, T, F, and B values.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The Frobenius cycle that ensures every split is matched by a corresponding join.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Lean 4 kernel fork that disables ex falso quodlibet to permit B-state stability.

phase_6.cycle_cost: The computational energy required to maintain the B-state against classical collapse.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: A state of unresolved contradiction between two distinct imscriptions.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A stable dialetheic object where the contradiction is held as a generative signal.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The Frobenius closure ensures that the information content of the split faces is perfectly preserved in the fused Janus gate.
